reasoning: The tool specifically identified a statistical probability of 0.68 for an increase over the threshold of 3.67. This serves as our primary anchor for the nearer date (Feb 8). The structural backdrop also lightly supports this upside: the Fed's January FOMC meeting resulted in unchanged rates, and market expectations for the next rate cut have drifted further back to structural points in 2026 (e.g. June). Furthermore, looking at the daily values leading up to submission, 3.67 acts as a frequent floor with occasional wicks up to 3.70 and 3.71, providing an asymmetric likelihood of observing a value strictly >3.67. For the later date (March 3), we pull the probabiltiy closer to 0.5 (to 0.59) as expanding time horizons introduce macroeconomic uncertainty, tracking closer toward market neutrality according to fundamental forecasting principles.
